title: Second Largest Number
statement:
Write a Python program that reads `n` integers (one per line) and prints the second largest unique number among them.

The first line of input is `n` (number of integers), followed by `n` integers each on a new line.

If all numbers are the same, print `None`.
starter_code:
n = int(input())
numbers = [int(input()) for _ in range(n)]

# Write your solution here
# Find and print the second largest unique number
# If not found, print None
constraints: Numbers can be negative or positive integers
Find the second largest among UNIQUE values
Print None (not 'None') if there is no second largest
